planning_target.planning_purpose: Plan v1.1 implementation work so the Rust CLI can perform image search plus artifact-backed image retrieval and validated delivery packaging.
planning_target.acceptance_object: TASK-007 final delivery acceptance task and planning output report
planning_target.planning_boundary: planning only; do not implement product changes or run generated task packages during planning

source_inventory.used_for: Product flow, Rust CLI boundary, provider/channel/VLM rules, and completion rules.
Rust 2021, clap, serde, thiserror, ureq, repository metadata, and verification commands.
v1.1 FR-001 through FR-015, AC-001 through AC-019, NFR and metrics.
Module boundaries, runtime sequence, ports, package architecture, and risks.
Accepted LLD task decomposition and design handoff structure.
Aggregate Rust-facing types, interfaces, state, package, validation, CLI, and test matrix.
Definition of complete v1.1 image search and artifact-backed retrieval delivery.
QueryPlan, config, policy, redaction, and retry counters.
BaseSearchProvider, SerpApi adapter, scheduling, candidate normalization, and provenance.
Candidate/image mechanical validation and Qwen 3.5 VLM subjective evaluation.
Artifact-backed retrieval, normal web fetch, source-page resolve, fallback, and policies.
Full run loop, package, validation, self-check, CLI, and exit codes.
Test matrix, fixture E2E, real-service smoke, blocked evidence, and acceptance report.
Accepted LLD design set, DAG, blockers, and development handoff verdict.
Real provider, Qwen, paid, robots, authorization, and quality calibration gates.
Existing Rust module layout and migration anchors.
Existing fixture/unit/integration tests to migrate to v1.1.

source_interpretation.current_phase: v1.1 development planning after accepted LLD design
source_interpretation.product_goal: Implement a Rust CLI that accepts QueryPlan, searches image candidates through default SerpApi provider, evaluates candidates, retrieves image artifacts through pluggable channels, evaluates retrieved images, and builds a validated delivery package.
capability_musts: SerpApi Google Images default search provider
Qwen 3.5 VLM direct subjective evaluation adapter
artifact-backed normal web retrieval with source-page fallback
local deterministic tests and real-service smoke or blocked evidence
implementation_gates: full run CLI and package validator

stages: STAGE-003 task logic and completeness review
STAGE-004 whole-plan integrity and dependency review

phase_reports.purpose: Extract v1.1 implementation obligations for image search plus artifact-backed retrieval from PRD/HLD/LLD and detailed designs.
outputs: Whole-plan outline for domain/config, SerpApi search, Qwen quality, artifact retrieval, full CLI/package flow, testing, acceptance

phase_reports.purpose: Decompose obligations into cohesive, independently acceptable development tasks.
Review every task for cohesion, completeness, acceptance, verification, stop conditions, and handoff value.
Review DAG, reverse requirement coverage, execution order, parallel safety, and final delivery acceptance.

tasks.title: QueryPlan config policy and shared domain foundation
allowed_scope: Implement v1.1 QueryPlanInput and NormalizedQueryPlan with required_image_count canonical field and required_count alias.
Implement RuntimeConfig DTOs for providers, retrieval channels, Qwen 3.5 VLM, policy, output, quality defaults, and execution limits.
Implement defaults and invariants: count=1, quality=general, candidate_target=20N, retrieval_batch_target=2N, retry_limit=3, full_attempt_limit=4.
Implement policy narrowing, paid disabled by default, robots warn/block posture, redaction helpers, and machine-readable admission/config diagnostics.
forbidden_scope: Do not call external search providers, retrieval channels, or Qwen 3.5 VLM.
Do not build delivery packages or implement CLI run orchestration.
Do not serialize resolved credentials, tokens, cookies, signed URLs, or private keys.
acceptance: Valid QueryPlan/config input produces NormalizedQueryPlan and RuntimeConfig with package-safe diagnostics.
All target derivation and retry/full-attempt invariants are represented by typed data and tests.
Policy config cannot silently broaden QueryPlan policy and cannot enable paid retrieval without explicit config and QueryPlan allowance.
Redaction removes credential-like values from diagnostics and package-safe projections.
stop_conditions: Stop if a source-backed schema change would alter PRD/LLD public QueryPlan or RuntimeConfig semantics.
Stop if a requested implementation would require storing resolved secrets in public DTOs.
Stop if cargo is unavailable and record the blocked command and residual risk.
handoff_requirements: NormalizedQueryPlan, RuntimeConfig, policy/redaction helpers, failure codes, and tests are ready for search, quality, retrieval, and orchestrator tasks.

tasks.title: Search provider registry scheduler and SerpApi adapter
inputs: TASK-001 NormalizedQueryPlan, RuntimeConfig.providers, redaction helpers, and provider policy.
Current src/search, src/domain/search.rs, src/domain/candidate.rs, and src/ports/mod.rs.
allowed_scope: Introduce or migrate to BaseSearchProvider with readiness, supported constraints, structured SearchRequest, and SearchResponse.
Implement provider registry from RuntimeConfig.providers with readiness reports and effective weighted scheduling.
Implement default serpapi_google_images adapter using endpoint https://serpapi.com/search, engine=google_images, and SERPAPI_API_KEY or configured credential env var.
Normalize SerpApi image_results[] into CandidateRecord with image URL, source page, thumbnail, rank, dimensions, MIME/license hints, provenance, dedupe key, and origin IDs.
Preserve deterministic RandomSource for scheduler tests and mark fixtures as fixture-only.
forbidden_scope: Do not retrieve images or mark candidates as accepted for delivery.
Do not perform candidate mechanical or VLM subjective evaluation.
Do not hard-code credentials or serialize resolved SERPAPI_API_KEY values.
Do not let fixture providers satisfy production readiness or production package evidence.
acceptance: At least one ready real provider can be scheduled when configured; missing SerpApi credentials produce PROVIDER_CREDENTIAL_MISSING readiness evidence.
SerpApi image_results[] fixtures normalize into package-safe CandidateRecord values with provenance and dedupe evidence.
Weighted scheduling preserves provider id, search round, rank, usage events, and shortage diagnostics.
Search output is ready for TASK-003 and image-recalls.json without leaking secrets.
stop_conditions: Stop if SerpApi response fields cannot be mapped without fabricating required candidate facts.
Stop if provider credential handling would leak resolved secrets into public DTOs, logs, or package evidence.
Stop if TASK-001 config/domain contracts are missing or incompatible.
handoff_requirements: SearchSessionOutcome, ProviderReadinessReport, normalized CandidateRecord, dedupe/usage diagnostics, and SerpApi adapter tests are ready for quality and orchestration.

tasks.title: Candidate and image quality with Qwen 3.5 VLM adapter
inputs: TASK-001 quality, policy, VlmEvaluationConfig, and redaction contracts.
TASK-002 SearchSessionOutcome and expanded CandidateRecord values.
allowed_scope: Implement typed mechanical blocking/reference metric facts for candidates and retrieved images.
Upgrade VlmEvaluationPort with readiness, evaluate_candidates, and evaluate_images structured DTOs.
Implement direct qwen_3_5_vlm adapter boundary using QWEN_API_TOKEN by default, runtime endpoint/base URL, model, timeout, and prompt templates.
Implement candidate decisions so only mechanical pass plus Qwen approve enters RetrievableCandidateBatch.
Implement the post-retrieval image acceptance API and decision logic over a stable artifact-evidence input DTO; TASK-005 invokes it after TASK-004 produces RetrievalBatchResult.
Keep fixture evaluator explicitly test-only and blocked in production.
forbidden_scope: Do not implement retrieval execution or artifact creation.
Do not treat mock/fixture VLM decisions as production acceptance.
Do not hard-code Qwen endpoint, model override, or token value.
Do not store raw Qwen credentials, raw unrestricted transcripts, or secret-bearing URLs.
acceptance: Candidate quality rejects mechanically blocked candidates before VLM and sends only mechanically passed candidates to Qwen in production.
Qwen unavailable, disabled, invalid response, missing decision, timeout, or fixture-in-production produces execution-blocked evidence.

Image acceptance API tests reject missing local/source artifact, sidecar, summary, task report, visual description, checksum, media mismatch, metadata-only, and ownership mismatch before VLM using fixture artifact-evidence inputs.
Quality outputs preserve trace links and expose candidate gates plus image acceptance API contracts for retrieval planning, orchestrator invocation, package review, and validation.
stop_conditions: Stop if Qwen API contract cannot be represented by the VlmEvaluationPort without inventing unsupported fields.
Stop if TASK-002 candidate fields or TASK-001 VLM config are missing.
Stop if implementation would require serializing QWEN_API_TOKEN or other resolved secrets.
handoff_requirements: CandidateQualityOutcome, RetrievableCandidateBatch, image acceptance API/decision types, VLM readiness reports, and quality diagnostics are ready for retrieval and orchestrator integration.

tasks.title: Artifact backed retrieval channels and fallback execution
inputs: TASK-001 retrieval policy, RuntimeConfig.retrieval_channels, redaction helpers, and retrieval_batch_target.
TASK-003 RetrievableCandidateBatch containing only candidates approved by mechanical and Qwen candidate gates.
allowed_scope: Implement RetrievalJob, RetrievalBatch, RetrievalArtifactResult, RetrievalBatchResult, attempt traces, fallback decisions, diagnostics, and channel readiness reports.
Migrate BaseRetrievalChannel to structured readiness and retrieve_batch returning RetrievalBatchResult.
Plan retrieval only from TASK-003 retrievable candidates and use retrieval_batch_target = required_image_count * 2.
Implement normal_web_fetch.direct_image_fetch and normal_web_fetch.source_page_resolve as separate attempt modes.

Generate local artifact, source artifact, source sidecar, content summary, task report, visual description, checksum, content-type evidence, dimensions, diagnostics, and fetch trace for complete results.
Implement self-hosted and paid channel boundaries/readiness; paid stays disabled unless runtime config and QueryPlan allow it.
forbidden_scope: Do not retrieve candidates that failed candidate mechanical or Qwen candidate gates.
Do not mark an image as delivered or semantically accepted.
Do not accept metadata-only, thumbnail-only, source-page-only, summary-only, sidecar-only, or task-report-only results as complete.
Do not bypass access restriction, robots blocker, prohibited source, login/paywall restriction, or paid-unconfirmed policy.
Do not let fixture retrieval satisfy production evidence.
acceptance: Every RetrievalStatus::Complete result has all artifact paths, checksum, content type, dimensions or diagnostic, media match, ownership, and trace evidence.
Fallback order is direct image fetch, source-page resolve, self-hosted service, paid online service, with policy blockers recorded.
Metadata-only and remote-only service responses are rejected, not accepted as image retrieval success.
Retrieval output is ready for TASK-005 to invoke the TASK-003 image acceptance API and then build/package validated delivery results.
stop_conditions: Stop if TASK-003 retrievable candidate contract is missing or bypassed.
Stop if policy decisions are required to run paid, login, paywalled, robots-blocked, or authorization-sensitive retrieval.
Stop if normal web fetch cannot produce complete local artifact evidence without expanding product scope.
handoff_requirements: RetrievalBatchResult and artifact evidence are ready for image acceptance, canonical package building, validation, and real-service smoke.

tasks.title: Full run orchestrator canonical package validation and CLI
inputs: TASK-001 NormalizedQueryPlan, RuntimeConfig, policy, retry counters.
TASK-003 CandidateQualityOutcome plus image acceptance API/decision types.
allowed_scope: Replace MVP run path with full workflow: admission, readiness, search, candidate quality, retrieval, image acceptance, package build, validation, review, and handoff.
Implement RunState, attempt records, retry invariants, accepted-image carry-forward, retry exhaustion, passed/partial/blocked status.

Build canonical package files and directories: image-recalls.json, retrieved-images.json, coverage-report.json, retrieval-manifest.json, package-summary.json, delivery-report.json, validation.json, review.json, handoff-report.json, images, evidence, diagnostics.
Implement PackageValidator and CLI validate-package; implement read-only inspect-package if practical for v1.1.
Upgrade self-check to report search, retrieval, Qwen 3.5 VLM, policy, output, credential, validator, and release blocker readiness.
Implement deterministic exit codes and JSON/human CLI output without secret leaks.
forbidden_scope: Do not redesign provider, Qwen, or retrieval internals owned by upstream tasks.
Do not mark production packages passed with fixture provider/channel/VLM evidence.
Do not deliver metadata-only, thumbnail-only, source-page-only, sidecar-only, summary-only, or task-report-only items.
Do not silently enable paid retrieval or bypass robots/authorization policy.
acceptance: image-retrieval run executes the full search + quality + retrieval + image acceptance + package + validation flow and no longer stops at planning output.
Package status is passed only when accepted images reach required count and validator passes; partial and blocked are evidence-backed.
validate-package reproduces deterministic validation failure codes for missing artifact, checksum, metadata-only delivery, retry mismatch, broken links, fixture production pass, and secret leaks.
self-check accurately reports readiness and blockers for SerpApi, artifact retrieval, Qwen 3.5 VLM, policy, output, and validator.
stop_conditions: Stop if any upstream task contract is missing or incompatible.
Stop if package validation cannot prove delivered images are local artifact-backed images.
Stop if CLI behavior would need undocumented product decisions.
handoff_requirements: A fixture-capable full CLI workflow and canonical package validator are ready for testing and real-service smoke harness.

tasks.title: Local test suite and real service smoke evidence
allowed_scope: Migrate and extend deterministic unit, integration, fixture E2E, package validation, CLI golden, and security tests for AC-001 through AC-019.
Add fixture configs, QueryPlans, provider responses, retrieval artifacts, package fixtures, and golden outputs under tests/fixtures/v1_1.
Add real-service smoke harness gated by IMAGE_RETRIEVAL_REAL_SMOKE=1 and configured env paths/credentials.

Emit blocked/skipped real-service evidence when credentials, Qwen endpoint, policy decisions, or release gates are absent; blocked/skipped smoke evidence is diagnostic only and cannot satisfy the expected-target completion claim.
Run and report cargo fmt, cargo clippy, cargo test, and smoke commands when available.
forbidden_scope: Do not add credentials or example secret values.
Do not mark fixture evidence, skipped smoke, or blocked real-service gates as production acceptance.
Do not change product behavior beyond narrow bug fixes needed to make tests reflect source-backed contracts.
Do not claim commands passed unless they actually ran in this task execution cycle.
acceptance: Every PRD AC-001 through AC-019 has test or acceptance evidence mapped in the testing report.
Fixture E2E proves passed, partial, blocked, missing artifact, metadata-only, paid-unconfirmed, access-restricted, secret leak, and production-fixture rejection paths.
Real-service smoke proves SerpApi search, non-fixture artifact retrieval, Qwen candidate/image evaluation, accepted image, validated package, and secret scan when prerequisites are present.
When prerequisites are absent, smoke emits machine-readable blocked/skipped evidence and explicitly marks full expected-target completion as not proven.
stop_conditions: Stop and record blocked evidence if cargo is unavailable.
Stop real-service smoke and write blocked/skipped report if IMAGE_RETRIEVAL_REAL_SMOKE is not set or required credentials/endpoints/policy decisions are absent.
Stop if tests reveal a source/design contradiction requiring PRD/HLD/LLD update.
handoff_requirements: Testing report, verification logs or blocked-command records, fixture packages, and real-service smoke report are ready for final acceptance; the handoff must clearly state whether real SerpApi + retrieval + Qwen evidence proved the expected target.

tasks.title: Final v1.1 delivery acceptance
inputs: All non-deferred TASK-001 through TASK-006 outputs, handoffs, verification logs, package evidence, testing report, real-service smoke report, and release-gate status.
allowed_scope: Review all task outputs against PRD/HLD/LLD and this development plan.

Verify requirement coverage, DAG completion, package output, local verification evidence, real-service smoke evidence, blockers, explicit release-waived notes that do not claim accepted completion, and no-new-scope compliance.
Write a final acceptance report with accepted, blocked, or rejected verdict. Only accepted means the v1.1 expected target is proven.
Record constrained defect findings discovered during acceptance; only fix documentation typos in the acceptance report itself.
forbidden_scope: Do not add new product scope, provider behavior, retrieval behavior, or tests except acceptance-report documentation.

Do not mark accepted when SerpApi, retrieval artifact, Qwen, package validation, or real-service smoke evidence is missing. A waiver may only produce blocked/release-waived language, not accepted completion.
Do not claim cargo or smoke commands passed unless evidence from TASK-006 exists.
acceptance: Acceptance report covers every FR/AC and states pass/fail/blocked/waived evidence.

Final verdict is blocked or rejected when required task output, verification, package validation, or real-service smoke evidence is missing; waiver language must not convert missing expected-target evidence into accepted completion.
Final verdict confirms image search + artifact-backed retrieval + Qwen acceptance + canonical package when accepted.
Unresolved paid, robots/site-rule, authorization, quality calibration, or environment blockers are recorded with explicit handling.
verification: Review TASK-006 verification logs and smoke reports
Review generated package with validate-package evidence
Verify acceptance-report.md content includes verdict, evidence, SerpApi, retrieval, Qwen, package, and real-service evidence status
stop_conditions: Stop and reject acceptance if any non-deferred task evidence is missing.
Stop and reject or require blocked/release-waived handling if real-service smoke is blocked and the release still claims full expected-target completion.
Stop if requirement coverage cannot be traced to implementation evidence.
handoff_requirements: Final acceptance report gives the next user or release owner a clear pass/fail verdict, evidence index, and remaining blockers.

edges.reason: Search provider work consumes NormalizedQueryPlan, RuntimeConfig.providers, policy, and redaction helpers.
Quality work consumes QualityRequirements, VlmEvaluationConfig, policy, and redaction helpers.
edges.consumed_output: QualityRequirements, VlmEvaluationConfig, policy config, redaction helpers

edges.reason: Candidate quality consumes normalized search candidates and provider provenance.
Retrieval consumes retrieval policy, channel config, redaction, and retrieval_batch_target.
Retrieval must plan only from candidates accepted by mechanical and Qwen candidate quality gates.
edges.consumed_output: RetrievableCandidateBatch with approved candidates and rejected decision evidence

edges.reason: Orchestrator consumes admitted query plan, runtime config, policy, and retry invariants.
Orchestrator calls search and packages image recall evidence.
Orchestrator calls candidate/image quality and packages VLM decisions.
edges.consumed_output: CandidateQualityOutcome plus image acceptance API/decision types

edges.reason: Orchestrator consumes retrieval artifacts for package and validation.
Testing requires the complete CLI workflow, package builder, validator, and self-check.
edges.consumed_output: Full run CLI, canonical package, package validator, self-check

edges.reason: Final acceptance depends on all implementation task evidence.
Final acceptance depends on all implementation task evidence.
Final acceptance depends on all implementation task evidence.
Final acceptance depends on all implementation task evidence.
Final acceptance depends on full workflow and package evidence.
Final acceptance consumes testing and smoke evidence.
edges.consumed_output: Testing report, verification logs, real-service smoke report

coverage_matrix.source_area: Image search capability with SerpApi default provider
verification: search integration tests and SerpApi fixture normalization

coverage_matrix.source_area: Candidate and image validation with Qwen 3.5 VLM
evidence: CandidateQualityOutcome and image acceptance API/decision types
RetrievalBatchResult with local artifacts and trace

verification: fmt/clippy/test plus real smoke or blocked evidence

requirement_coverage_matrix.requirement_family: CLI run workflow and retry/package delivery
Search providers and SerpApi default provider
Candidate and image quality with Qwen 3.5 VLM

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Build the v1.1 input, config, policy, and shared domain foundation.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: src/domain/query_plan.rs, src/domain/config.rs, src/domain/policy.rs, src/domain/mod.rs, src/policy/mod.rs, src/error/mod.rs, tests/domain_baseline_test.rs.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-001-queryplan-config-policy-design.md.
Expected output paths are src/domain/query_plan.rs, src/domain/config.rs, src/domain/policy.rs, src/domain/mod.rs, src/policy/mod.rs, src/error/mod.rs, tests/domain_baseline_test.rs.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: Valid QueryPlan/config input produces NormalizedQueryPlan and RuntimeConfig with package-safe diagnostics.; All target derivation and retry/full-attempt invariants are represented by typed data and tests..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Implement pluggable image search with SerpApi as the default broad provider.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: src/search, src/domain/search.rs, src/domain/candidate.rs, src/ports/mod.rs, tests/search_integration_test.rs.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_surface: Search provider contract, scheduler, SerpApi normalization, and readiness tests.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-002-search-provider-candidate-design.md.
Expected output paths are src/ports/mod.rs, src/domain/search.rs, src/domain/candidate.rs, src/search, tests/search_integration_test.rs.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: At least one ready real provider can be scheduled when configured; missing SerpApi credentials produce PROVIDER_CREDENTIAL_MISSING readiness evidence.; SerpApi image_results[] fixtures normalize into package-safe CandidateRecord values with provenance and dedupe evidence..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Implement mechanical and Qwen subjective gates for candidates and images.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: src/quality, src/domain/image.rs, src/domain/metrics.rs, src/ports/mod.rs, tests/candidate_quality_test.rs.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-003-quality-vlm-design.md.
Expected output paths are src/quality, src/domain/image.rs, src/domain/metrics.rs, src/ports/mod.rs, tests/candidate_quality_test.rs.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: Candidate quality rejects mechanically blocked candidates before VLM and sends only mechanically passed candidates to Qwen in production.; Qwen unavailable, disabled, invalid response, missing decision, timeout, or fixture-in-production produces execution-blocked evidence..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Implement retrieval channels that actually fetch images and produce complete artifact evidence.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: src/retrieval, src/domain/retrieval.rs, src/ports/mod.rs, tests/retrieval_test.rs.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_surface: Retrieval artifact, fallback, policy, and evidence tests.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-004-retrieval-artifact-channel-design.md.
Expected output paths are src/retrieval, src/domain/retrieval.rs, src/ports/mod.rs, tests/retrieval_test.rs.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: Every RetrievalStatus::Complete result has all artifact paths, checksum, content type, dimensions or diagnostic, media match, ownership, and trace evidence.; Fallback order is direct image fetch, source-page resolve, self-hosted service, paid online service, with policy blockers recorded..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Wire all implemented capabilities into the CLI delivery workflow and package contract.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: src/main.rs, src/orchestrator, src/delivery, src/self_check, src/validation, src/domain/delivery.rs, tests/e2e_fixture_test.rs.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_surface: CLI, orchestrator, package builder, validator, and fixture E2E tests.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-005-orchestrator-package-validation-cli-design.md.
Expected output paths are src/main.rs, src/orchestrator, src/delivery, src/self_check, src/validation, src/domain/delivery.rs, tests/e2e_fixture_test.rs.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: image-retrieval run executes the full search + quality + retrieval + image acceptance + package + validation flow and no longer stops at planning output.; Package status is passed only when accepted images reach required count and validator passes; partial and blocked are evidence-backed..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Prove the implemented search and retrieval workflow with deterministic and real-service evidence.
cohesion_basis: Owns one bounded output cluster: tests, tests/fixtures/v1_1, tasks/development/v1.1/testing-report.md, tasks/development/v1.1/real-service-smoke-report.json.
Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_surface: Testing report, cargo command results, fixture E2E, and real-service smoke evidence.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-006-testing-real-service-acceptance-design.md, RELEASE_GATES.md.
Expected output paths are tests, tests/fixtures/v1_1, tasks/development/v1.1/testing-report.md, tasks/development/v1.1/real-service-smoke-report.json.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: Every PRD AC-001 through AC-019 has test or acceptance evidence mapped in the testing report.; Fixture E2E proves passed, partial, blocked, missing artifact, metadata-only, paid-unconfirmed, access-restricted, secret leak, and production-fixture rejection paths..
Verification includes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

task_reviews.primary_responsibility: Decide whether v1.1 genuinely satisfies image search plus retrieval acceptance.
cohesion_basis: Does not perform work owned by upstream or downstream task IDs.
acceptance_evidence: Source refs define the responsibility: docs/v1.1/PRD.md, docs/v1.1/HLD.md, docs/v1.1/LLD.md, docs/design/v1.1-TASK-007-detailed-design-acceptance-review.md, tasks/development/v1.1/development-planning.json.
Expected output paths are tasks/development/v1.1/acceptance-report.md.

Acceptance criteria are task-scoped: Acceptance report covers every FR/AC and states pass/fail/blocked evidence.; accepted is allowed only when required task output, verification, package validation, and real-service smoke evidence prove the expected target.

Verification includes: Review tasks/development/v1.1/development-planning.json; Review all TASK-001..TASK-006 handoffs; verify acceptance-report.md content includes verdict, evidence, SerpApi, retrieval, Qwen, package, and real-service evidence status.
Source refs, expected output paths, verification commands, and acceptance criteria are recorded for every task.
Reverse source requirement coverage maps FR/AC IDs to tasks, verification, and required evidence.
Task package fixtures provide executor-ready source, boundary, verification, and handoff instructions.
review_reasoning: Each task owns one bounded implementation or verification surface.
TASK-004 explicitly depends on TASK-003 so retrieval only operates on quality-approved candidates.
TASK-007 is the single final delivery acceptance task and depends on every non-deferred task.

standards: source traceability from PRD/HLD/LLD to implementation evidence
current-phase FR/AC coverage and no new product scope
all non-deferred task DAG outputs complete with handoff evidence
verification evidence for cargo fmt, cargo clippy, cargo test, fixture E2E, package validation, and real-service smoke; blocked smoke evidence is diagnostic and cannot prove accepted completion
downstream handoff readiness through canonical package and acceptance report
methods: review development-planning.json and every task package
review all TASK-001 through TASK-006 handoff evidence and changed output paths
run or review verification command logs and blocked-command records
review generated package, validate-package report, testing-report.md, real-service-smoke-report.json, and release gates
evidence_required: tasks/development/v1.1/TASKS.md and every task package directory
canonical package files and validate-package evidence
verification_commands_or_checks: review source traceability and requirement coverage matrix
verify acceptance-report.md content includes verdict, evidence, SerpApi, retrieval, Qwen, package, and real-service evidence status
pass_fail_rules: fail if required evidence is missing
fail if any required verification command failed without blocker record
fail if any current-phase FR/AC is uncovered
fail accepted completion if SerpApi search, artifact retrieval, Qwen evaluation, real-service smoke, or package validation evidence is missing
fail if unresolved blockers are not recorded as required fixes or explicit blocked/release-waived notes
fail if implementation scope exceeds the plan
blocker_conditions: missing task handoff or dependency output
failed cargo verification or package validation
missing SerpApi credentials or adapter readiness without blocked evidence
missing Qwen 3.5 VLM endpoint/token/readiness without blocked evidence
real-service smoke blocked while release claims full expected-target completion
unresolved paid/robots/authorization/quality gate without warning, blocker, or waiver

coverage_findings: FR-001 through FR-015 and AC-001 through AC-019 are covered by implementation/testing/final acceptance tasks.
Image search plus artifact retrieval is covered directly by TASK-002 and TASK-004 and integrated by TASK-005/TASK-006.
dependency_findings: TASK-004 depends on TASK-003, preventing retrieval of unevaluated search candidates.
TASK-005 depends on all implementation capability tasks before integrating the full workflow.
TASK-007 depends on every non-deferred task and is last.
parallel_safety_findings: No parallel groups are declared because the accepted LLD chain has direct/transitive dependencies between all capability tasks.
execution_order_findings: Execution order TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004, TASK-005, TASK-006, TASK-007 is topological and matches the product flow.
delivery_acceptance_findings: TASK-007 has concrete report path, evidence, verification checks, binary pass/fail rules, and blocker conditions.

planning_gate_report.evidence: PRD/HLD/LLD and accepted detailed design documents are inventoried.
Tasks and reverse coverage matrix cite PRD/HLD/LLD and detailed design refs.
In-scope Rust CLI image search + retrieval work and out-of-scope Web UI/SaaS/image generation work are explicit.
Cargo.toml confirms Rust 2021, clap, serde, thiserror, ureq.
Each task owns one output cluster and one acceptance surface.
Each task has expected outputs, verification, stop conditions, and handoff requirements.
All four phase reports are present and passing.
cycle_check.status=pass and topological execution order is recorded.
No parallel groups declared due direct/transitive dependencies.
Every task has PROMPT.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and spec.yaml.
Every spec.yaml is a 2.0 pointer contract.
TASK-007 is the only delivery_acceptance task and is last.
delivery_acceptance defines standards, methods, evidence, checks, pass/fail rules, and blockers.
Only task-scoped prompts under task folders are generated.

external_dependencies.dependency: SerpApi Google Images service and credentials
external_dependencies.planning_handling: TASK-002 implements adapter and readiness. TASK-006 real smoke requires SERPAPI_API_KEY or configured equivalent, otherwise blocked evidence is required.
TASK-003 implements adapter/readiness. TASK-006 real smoke requires QWEN_API_TOKEN and endpoint/model config, otherwise blocked evidence is required.
TASK-001/TASK-004 keep paid disabled by default and TASK-006 records blocked/skipped paid smoke unless explicitly allowed.
TASK-001/TASK-004/TASK-005 expose warning/blocker posture; no silent bypass is allowed.
TASK-001/TASK-003/TASK-006 record defaults, calibration evidence, or waiver before final acceptance.

open_questions: Will v1.1 release enable paid retrieval smoke, or should paid channels remain disabled-only?
Should unresolved robots/site-rule posture default to warning or blocker for production smoke?
What Qwen 3.5 VLM endpoint/base URL and model id will production config use?
